title: Vanilla cupcakes
subtitle: Light vanilla sponge with vanilla bean buttercream
excerpt: Twelve light vanilla sponge cupcakes with a swirled vanilla bean buttercream. Baked in a bun tin, cooled, and piped. The sponge is a scaled version of the Victoria sandwich method. Makes twelve.
sourceNotes: Mrs Beeton, Book of Household Management (1861), Project Gutenberg #10136: small plain cakes baked in individual moulds are documented in the cake chapter as a domestic preparation, made by the creaming method with flour, butter, sugar, and eggs. The Victoria sandwich batter scaled to individual moulds produces the standard British cupcake. The buttercream frosting is documented in Eliza Acton, Modern Cookery for Private Families (1845), Project Gutenberg, as a plain icing of icing sugar beaten with butter, used for small fancy cakes.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze unfrosted baked cupcakes in a bag for up to 2 months. Frost after thawing.
recipe.batchNotes: Double and bake in two tins.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Bake and cool the cupcakes a day ahead. Frost on the day of serving.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven, middle shelf; the cupcakes are done when the tops spring back to a light press and a skewer through the centre comes out clean

content.text: Cupcakes are the Victoria sandwich batter scaled to individual cases. The same rules apply: all ingredients at room temperature, butter creamed until pale, eggs added gradually. Fill each case no more than two-thirds full; the batter rises as it bakes and overfilled cases spill and dome unevenly.

Preheat the oven to 180°C fan. Line a 12-hole bun tin with paper cases. Beat the {{unsalted-butter}} and {{caster-sugar}} until pale and light; about 3 minutes with a hand mixer. Add the {{eggs}} one at a time with the {{vanilla-extract}}, beating after each. Fold in the {{self-raising-flour}} and {{sea-salt-fine}} with a spatula, then add the {{whole-milk}} and fold until just combined. Divide evenly between the cases — an ice-cream scoop keeps the amounts consistent. Bake for 18–20 minutes until the tops spring back when pressed. Cool in the tin for 5 minutes, then transfer to a rack and cool completely before frosting.

Beat the remaining {{unsalted-butter}} until very pale and soft; at least 3 minutes. Add the sifted {{icing-sugar}} in three additions, beating after each. Add the {{vanilla-bean-paste}} and {{whole-milk}} and beat for 2 more minutes until light and fluffy. Fit a piping bag with a star nozzle, fill with buttercream, and pipe a swirl starting at the outer edge and working inward to a peak. Hold the bag upright and consistent pressure through the full circle produces an even swirl.
